completed_tasks.name: Fix populationCap initialization to 5 in gameState.js
Import checkTechUnlocks and wire 2 call sites in GameScene.js

human_actions_pending.context: Sprint-5 commits (and earlier) are unpushed -- non-blocking but should be done before demo

human_actions_pending.action: Remove DevMode panel before public demo
human_actions_pending.context: DevMode panel (G key toggle in index.html + GameScene.js) is a test utility. Must be removed before any public or demo build. Currently hidden by default but the code is present.

decisions.rationale: After Carpentry: T1 dims, T2 auto-selects. After Masonry: T1+T2 dim, T3 auto-selects. Lower tiers are never simultaneously available with higher tiers.

decisions.decision: Visual tier: existing buildings upgrade, new placements show selected tier
decisions.rationale: applyBuildingVisual accepts overrideVisualTier; pass building.tier on fresh placement so player sees what they paid for. refreshAllBuildingVisuals (called on unlock) uses getVisualTier to upgrade pre-existing buildings.

decisions.decision: DevMode panel as first-class testing tool
decisions.rationale: G key toggles panel with resource injection and tech force/remove toggles. Built during QA to unblock Masonry testing (can't reach ike>=3 in normal play quickly).

decisions.decision: Testing workflow (seed states + agent audit) deferred
decisions.rationale: User requested deferral -- test infrastructure is Sprint 6+ scope, not Sprint 6 necessarily.

decisions.decision: Masonry threshold 3 ike is a placeholder
decisions.rationale: Calibrated to current annual ike model (1/year). Needs revision when quarterly ike cadence (4/year) is implemented.

next_action: Sprint 6 planning. Define game feature scope (what comes after tech unlocks -- resource collection, population growth, Makahiki loop, or another feature). Testing workflow infrastructure is deferred but captured.
context_notes: Sprint 5 is fully complete and verified. All commits are on master. The project is at a clean stopping point between sprints. No in-flight work.
